metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC1's grep command to exclude the two known out-of-scope files (docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md) by adding a pathspec exclusion, OR remove `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` from the pattern and document it as a separate check, OR expand the allowed-hits whitelist to explicitly name those two files so the pass condition matches the command's actual output.
findings.finding: AC1's grep pattern includes `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` but the plan explicitly carves out docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md as containing 'fast-forward only' for a *different* concept that is out of scope — so the grep command AS WRITTEN will produce hits in those two files, causing the AC to fail even when all required work is correctly done. The allowed-hits whitelist in the AC prose does not include those two files, creating an irreconcilable conflict between the command and its stated pass condition.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add `--exclude-dir` / pathspec negation to the grep, for example:
```
git grep -icE 'autosubmit|auto-?lander|land-contract|land-status|lander_down|fast[- ]forward[- ]only|\bFFO\b|rebase[- ]on[- ]behalf|land <change>|--wait|both votes green' \
  -- '*.md' \
  ':(exclude)docs/adr/0010*.md' \
  ':(exclude)docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md'
```
Then the whitelist in the prose (only 0040, 0042, 0047, 0025, 0041, and hyperlinks) matches what the command can actually produce.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC1's allowlist with the Scope Notes carve-out: either extend AC1's grep allowlist to explicitly include `docs/adr/0010` and `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` for the `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` pattern, or narrow the grep pattern to exclude that term (since only autolander-specific language is being removed).
findings.finding: AC1's grep pattern includes `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` and says hits are allowed ONLY in the two superseded ADRs and a narrow allowlist — but Scope Notes separately state that 'GitHub-mirror "fast-forward only" in docs/adr/0010 + docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md is a different concept and out of scope.' Those two files are NOT in AC1's allowlist, so an implementer running AC1 verbatim will get unexpected hits from those files and incorrectly conclude the criterion fails.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 (old-model ABSENCE grep) vs. Scope Notes paragraph 3
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC1 allowlist parenthetical to read: '…and the ADR-0047 filename inside hyperlinks. (Also allowed: `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` hits in docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, which refer to the unrelated GitHub push setting — see Scope Notes.)' Or split the grep into two commands: one for the autolander-specific terms (no fast-forward) and a separate one for the FFO/fast-forward terms with a broader allowlist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the AC1 contradiction with docs/land-contract.md: either (a) declare d0e5 (land-contract.md deletion) as a sequencing prerequisite that must complete before this story's AC1 can pass, or (b) explicitly add docs/land-contract.md to the AC1 allowlist with a rationale note (e.g. 'historical contract doc, to be deleted by d0e5'), or (c) include deleting/tombstoning docs/land-contract.md in this story's scope.
findings.finding: AC1's pass condition (old-model grep hits appear ONLY in the two superseded ADRs, ADR 0047, and pointer-note ADRs) will not be satisfied as long as docs/land-contract.md remains in the repo, because that file contains multiple AC1-triggering terms (lander_down, Autosubmit, land-status, 'land <change>', FFO). The plan explicitly defers docs/land-contract.md deletion to sibling story d0e5 and does not name d0e5 as a prerequisite or add docs/land-contract.md to the AC1 allowlist — creating a contradiction: AC1 requires old-model terms absent from non-exempted files, but a live non-exempted file (docs/land-contract.md) will still contain them.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; Scope notes ('infra/ autolander references are sibling bcf0's scope')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope notes: 'docs/land-contract.md still contains old-model terms (lander_down, Autosubmit, land-status, land <change>); this story does NOT delete it (that is d0e5). AC1 therefore requires d0e5 to complete first, OR add docs/land-contract.md to the AC1 exemption list alongside the superseded ADRs.' Then update AC1's allowlist to include docs/land-contract.md with the qualifier 'pending deletion by d0e5'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying the pointer note was added to docs/adr/0025 and docs/adr/0041, e.g. `grep -l 'submit type is now Rebase-If-Necessary' docs/adr/0025-*.md docs/adr/0041-*.md` listing both files.
findings.finding: The 'What' section lists docs/adr/0025 and docs/adr/0041 as deliverables ('Light pointer-note … prior-model logic left for a separate review'), but no AC verifies that the pointer notes were actually added to those files — AC1 only checks that old terms are absent, not that the new pointer note is present.
findings.location: What section ('Banner-only / light pointer-note' paragraph) vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC3b: `grep -l 'Rebase-If-Necessary' docs/adr/0025-*.md docs/adr/0041-*.md` must list both files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC-1 to exclude docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md from the grep (e.g., add `-- ':!docs/adr/0010-*.md' ':!docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md'` pathspec exclusions) or explicitly add them to the allowed-survivors list with a note that their 'fast-forward only' is a different concept.
findings.finding: The AC-1 grep allowlist does not include docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, yet the plan's own 'Scope notes' parenthetical acknowledges those files contain 'fast-forward only' (a different concept, explicitly out of scope). The AC as written will spuriously fail after a correct implementation because those files are not carved out of the grep pattern or the expected-survivors list.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet (Old-model ABSENCE grep AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC-1 grep with: `git grep -icE "autosubmit|auto-?lander|land-contract|land-status|lander_down|fast[- ]forward[- ]only|\bFFO\b|rebase[- ]on[- ]behalf|land <change>|--wait|both votes green" -- '*.md' ':!docs/adr/0010-*.md' ':!docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md'` and update the expected-survivors list to document why those two files are excluded.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the 'Files rewritten' list: remove or replace the `land-contract.md` bullet (line 108) and the 'auto-lander's agent contract' description with a pointer to the new plain-Submit landing model.
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` (the docs-index file at docs/README.md, distinct from the top-level README.md) contains old-model prose on line 108 — `"the auto-lander's agent contract"` plus a live hyperlink to `land-contract.md` — but is not listed among the plan's 'Files rewritten' targets. The `auto-?lander` term in its body will cause AC1's grep to return an unexpected hit, and the dangling `land-contract.md` link will cause `check_docs_index.py --check` (AC4) to fail once sibling d0e5 deletes `land-contract.md`.
findings.location: What — 'Files rewritten' list; docs/README.md line 108; AC1 grep; AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the docs/README.md bullet at line 108 with a pointer to the relevant section of CONTRIBUTING.md or docs/your-first-change.md describing the new Submit-when-green flow, and remove the reference to `land-contract.md` entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add docs/README.md to the explicit list of files to update: remove or update the land-contract.md entry (line 108) so it no longer describes the retired auto-lander contract as an active resource, satisfying AC1's grep for 'auto-lander'.
findings.finding: The plan lists 'README.md (landing pointer)' as a rewrite target but does not address docs/README.md (the docs index), which at line 108 describes land-contract.md as an active live resource: '**[land-contract.md](land-contract.md)** — the auto-lander's agent contract: the `land` / `land-status` outcome enum…'. After this story's sweep, that entry will be a dangling reference to a stale doc (land-contract.md still exists but describes the retired model), and 'docs/README.md' is not listed in the plan's files-rewritten list.
findings.location: What section — 'Files rewritten to the new model'; docs/README.md line 108
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Files rewritten to the new model' list, add: 'docs/README.md (remove or tombstone the land-contract.md entry in the Contributor index — it references the auto-lander as an active contract)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either expand the parent epic's docs-child SC to explicitly include ADR 0025 and ADR 0041 pointer-notes, or remove those edits from this leaf's scope and place them in the appropriate sibling (ADR story) that the parent already authorises for ADR work.
findings.finding: The leaf introduces 'light pointer-notes' edits to ADR 0025 and ADR 0041, which are not mentioned anywhere in the parent epic's plan, success criteria, or scope notes. The parent's ADR SC explicitly and exhaustively names only ADR 0047 (create), ADR 0040 (supersede), and ADR 0042 (supersede). Touching ADR 0025 and ADR 0041 is new scope the parent does not contain.
findings.location: Plan section: 'Banner-only (historical decision records…)' / 'Light pointer-note' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to the parent epic's docs SC: 'ADR 0025 and ADR 0041 receive a brief pointer-note (submit type is now Rebase-If-Necessary; prior model logic left for a separate review).' This makes the leaf a strict subset of the parent and closes the G7 gap without removing useful work.

findings.finding: The plan's AC1 ('a fresh grep returns only (a) historical ADRs with a supersession banner, and (b) ADR 0047 itself') is unachievable if infra/ script files remain in the repo after this story lands. Grep confirmed that infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/scripts/config-check.sh, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, and infra/compose/docker-compose.yml all contain 'autosubmit', 'autolander', or 'auto-lander' references that are INFRASTRUCTURE artifacts (not docs/guidance) belonging to the bcf0 decommission story — NOT this docs-sweep story. The plan does not state that AC1 should exclude infra/ infrastructure hits, leaving the acceptance criterion ambiguous and likely failing on execution if bcf0 has not already landed.
findings.location: AC1 / What section ('Targets include... grep is source of truth')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: '(infra/ script/config files referencing autolander are excluded from this grep gate — their removal is covered by the bcf0 decommission story, which is a prerequisite of this story)' and add 'Requires: bcf0 landed' to the plan's Prerequisites section.

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)' as a named sweep target, but CLAUDE.local.md is listed in .gitignore and does not exist in the repository. There is no file to sweep. The plan cannot update a gitignored, non-existent file.
findings.location: What section — 'CLAUDE.md (Git workflow / land-through-Gerrit section), CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)' with a note: 'CLAUDE.local.md is .gitignored and developer-local — not sweepable; add a note in CLAUDE.md instructing developers to update their local copy if they have one.'

findings.finding: CLAUDE.local.md is listed as a named edit target in the What section and AC2, but it is git-ignored (`.gitignore` line 14) and therefore does not exist as a committed repo file. It is a per-developer local file that cannot be edited as part of a repo change, so the plan's 'coordinate a rewrite of CLAUDE.local.md' is a hallucinated edit target for version-control purposes.
findings.location: ## What — comprehensive grep-driven sweep, bullet 1 ('CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)'); AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Remove CLAUDE.local.md from AC2 and add a sentence to CLAUDE.md's Git workflow section noting that any local CLAUDE.local.md should delete or replace its `lander_down` / `land <change> --wait` section.

findings.finding: AC4 says 'coordinate with the retire-code story' for CLI --help / MCP reference doc regeneration, but names no ticket, does not state whether AC4 blocks this story's completion or is conditional, and provides no in-session proving command the executor can run. An agent cannot verify or complete this criterion without knowing the sibling story's identity and status.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'Any CLI --help / drift-gated command+MCP reference docs regenerate clean after the land command is removed (coordinate with the retire-code story).'
findings.suggested_fix: AC4 (revised): 'Any CLI --help / drift-gated command+MCP reference docs regenerate clean after the land command is removed. **Depends on: [retire-code story ticket ID].** Proving command: `<binary> --help 2>&1 | grep -iE "autosubmit|land command" | wc -l` — expect 0. This AC may be verified in the retire-code story's PR; mark this story complete only after that PR merges.'

findings.finding: AC2 specifies that named files 'describe the Submit-when-green / Gerrit-rebases model' but provides no concrete proving command to verify PRESENCE of the new model's key facts in each file. Only AC1 checks for the ABSENCE of old terms via grep, leaving the positive claim in AC2 unverified mechanically.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE.local.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, your-first-change.md, README.md landing sections describe the Submit-when-green / Gerrit-rebases model.'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'Verified by: `grep -lE "LLM-Review|Verified\\+1|Rebase-If-Necessary" CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.local.md CONTRIBUTING.md docs/your-first-change.md README.md` — all six files must appear in output.'

findings.finding: AC#4 says 'coordinate with the retire-code story' for CLI --help regeneration but does not establish a hard sequencing dependency on the retire-code story (d0e5) completing first. ADR 0047's migration sequences this doc story (step 4) after code deletion (step 2), but if this story lands before d0e5, docs will assert 'no land command / no Autosubmit label' while the code and label still exist; if it lands after, the story's own sweep references docs/land-contract.md that has already been deleted by d0e5 — an ordering assumption that can silently break the sweep. 'Coordinate' is too loose to prevent either failure mode.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC#4; ## What — 'Targets include… docs/land-contract.md (delete/redirect)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'Prerequisite: story d0e5 (delete auto-lander code) must be closed before this sweep begins — the grep will then find no live `land` / `land-status` / `land-contract` artifacts in code, confirming the code story is complete, and the doc sweep removes their references.' Add `blocks: d0e5` → wait for d0e5 to close, then claim this story.

findings.finding: AC4 says 'Any CLI --help / drift-gated command + MCP reference docs regenerate clean after the land command is removed (coordinate with the retire-code story)' but names no specific story ticket and does not state whether this is a blocking dependency of this story or a follow-on. The 'land' command exists in infra/autolander/land.py today. AC4's completion depends on the retire-code story (d0e5) having removed the land command first, but the plan gives no evidence that d0e5 is sequenced as a prerequisite — it is merely mentioned as a coordination point.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC4 as: 'Requires d0e5 (retire-code) to have landed first. Once d0e5 is complete: re-run `rebar mcp-reference` and `rebar cli-reference` generation and confirm no land/land-status/land-contract entries appear in the output.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
